title: Repair related-test retrieval and compression evaluation
goal: Remove the live-goldset regression by incorporating retrieval-resolved test paths as an explicit non-graph evidence class, fixing path projection hygiene, and evaluating context compression without weakening recall or authority boundaries.

approval.note: Use an isolated Lenskit branch/worktree. Preserve the existing read-only and non-authoritative boundaries. Re-run the pre-registered live goldset before any promotion decision.

acceptance.assertion: Test-like repository paths from the already resolved query projection are emitted as related-test candidates with evidence_type=resolved_query and their citation/range metadata; they are never represented as graph edges, runtime dependencies or test coverage.

Graph-edge, symbol-index, resolved-query and heuristic candidates remain distinguishable, deterministic and precedence-ordered; stronger evidence does not erase weaker evidence classes unless an explicitly tested dedupe rule applies.
Evaluation path projections reject empty and non-repository-relative path values so context-size metrics contain only meaningful candidates.

The evaluator records aggregate and per-case context-path reduction. Utility may be established only with no recall regression and either the pre-existing recall-advantage threshold or at least 20 percent context reduction at equal-or-better recall; default_promoted remains false.

The same three pre-registered live cases are rerun on coherent bundles. Grabowski must recover tests/test_job_finalizer.py, no case may regress against baseline recall, and all commits, manifests, run ids and canonical digests are recorded.
RepoBrief remains read-only and produces no source, Git, patch, test, pull-request, snapshot or memory mutation and no review, coverage or merge authority.
Tests, all CI gates, full downloadable diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, mergeability and post-merge validation pass before verification.

metadata.outcome: The live recall regression is repaired. Resolved query test paths remain a separate navigation-evidence class, unsafe paths fail closed, and the fixed three-repository goldset preserves recall while materially reducing exposed context. The surface remains opt-in.

verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-resolved-tests: Lenskit PR #996 adds evidence_type=resolved_query with citation_id, source_range and range_status while explicitly denying graph and coverage authority.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-evidence-order: Tests preserve graph_edge, symbol_index_path_match, resolved_query and heuristic ordering; only pure heuristics are suppressed when resolved test evidence exists.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-path-hygiene: Raw empty, absolute, backslash, duplicate-slash, dot and parent segments are rejected before path normalization and excluded from metrics.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-compression-metric: The evaluator records case and aggregate context reduction and requires no recall regression before either recall or compression can establish bounded utility.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-live-rerun: The same Lenskit, Grabowski and Weltgewebe cases reached recall 1.0 with no regression; canonical context reduction was 50 percent and the stricter independent confirmation measured 32 percent.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-boundary: RepoBrief remains read-only and resolved_query remains derived navigation evidence without runtime, coverage, review or merge authority.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-merge-gates: PR #996 was mergeable, all head-bound CI gates passed, the complete diff was reviewed at SHA-256 00d408..., the P2 thread was resolved and merge commit dff582f... is present on Lenskit main.
